behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mocked diffs could pass while real GitHub diffs cause filtering failures due to format edge cases (off-by-one line numbers, hunk boundaries, context lines). Real failure: the analyzer includes unchanged lines or misses changed lines because the filtering logic makes incorrect assumptions about diff format. Testing with realistic GitHub diff format (fixture or API response) is required to catch line-mapping boundary errors.
behaviors.test_description: Create a PR with multiple files and multiple changed regions per file. Verify the analysis result includes only the changed line ranges and does not analyze unchanged regions. Compare actual change ranges (from diff) against lines included in analysis findings.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Unit tests mocking the GitHub API will not catch subtle failures in line number mapping. GitHub's API requires specific commit/path/line parameters, and the meaning of 'line' differs between unified diff format and GitHub's diff view. Real failures include: off-by-one calculation of diff line numbers, incorrect mapping between original/modified file line numbers, or mishandling of context vs. changed lines. Testing against realistic GitHub diff structure (API fixtures or real API) is needed to verify correct positioning.
behaviors.test_description: Post a review comment for a specific changed line and verify it appears on that exact line in GitHub's PR diff view, not on a neighboring unchanged line or wrong file. Test edge cases: first/last line of diff, multi-hunk files, and range comments if supported.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: This tests the core analysis capability. If the analyzer is deterministic (rule-based static analysis), Integration is sufficient—tests verify the detection logic works. If the analyzer is non-deterministic (LLM-based), mocking the LLM hides real detection failures and Agentic becomes necessary. Without certainty of implementation, Integration is the practical minimum that catches deterministic failures. However, if the system uses an LLM, only Agentic testing exposes actual analysis capability. Real failure: detection logic misses or misclassifies the injected bug.
behaviors.test_description: Create a PR with a known injected bug (e.g., unhandled null pointer, missing input validation, incorrect error handling). Run the PR review system and verify that at least one finding identifies the bug. The finding should accurately describe the defect, not just vague suggestions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Will catch rule-based detection failures reliably; Agentic tests needed if system uses LLM analysis.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: Unit tests of the summary-building logic could pass even if posting inline comments fails or succeeds only partially. Real failure: a partial failure where some inline comments post successfully and others fail, but the summary is still generated and includes the failed findings (inconsistency). Mocking the comment POST step would hide this scenario. Integration test must actually post inline comments (or test against a stateful mock) and verify the summary includes only findings whose inline comments were successfully posted.
behaviors.test_description: Run PR review on code with multiple distinct findings. Verify all inline comments were posted. Then verify the summary comment lists exactly those same findings in the same order, with no additions or omissions. Optionally, simulate an inline comment failure (e.g., throttle or permission error) and verify that finding does not appear in the summary.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This tests complete workflow orchestration including real GitHub API calls (read PR metadata, post inline comments, post summary). Real failures include: partial failures (PR read succeeds but comment posting fails), API rate limits, race conditions, network errors, or GitHub API changes between steps. Unit and Integration tests of individual steps could all pass while the full sequence fails due to orchestration bugs or real-world API constraints. System-level testing with actual GitHub API (or realistically-mocked HTTP responses with network behavior) is required. If the analysis step (behavior 3) involves LLM non-determinism, this becomes Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real test PR (or use a designated test repository). Run the full PR review automation end-to-end. Verify all three components succeed: (1) diff is read and analyzed, (2) inline comments appear on PR, (3) summary comment appears on PR. Verify no partial failures (all-or-nothing per step). Monitor for timeouts, rate-limit errors, and API errors.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System level assumes analysis is deterministic. If analysis is LLM-based (Agentic), this becomes Workflow (multiple agentic steps in sequence).
